ocr.text: The underwriting profit margins and life insurance persistency metrics presented in Table 1 provide critical insights into the
performance of the life insurance sector within the Asia-Pacific region.
Year  TotalRevenue(CNY)  UnderwritingProfit(CNY)  ProfitMargin(%)  YoYChange(%)  MarketShare(%)  ClaimsRatio(%)
As markets continue to evolve, the ability to maintain high persistency rates becomes pivotal for insurers aiming to enhance
long-term profitability. In 2024, the persistency rate reached an impressive 87%, reflecting a robust commitment from
policyholders and contributing to the overall stability of underwriting profit margins. Notably, in 2023, the underwriting profit
margin stood at 5.7%, a slight shift from the previous year's margin of 5.3%. This marginal fluctuation is indicative of the
challenges faced by insurers in balancing competitive pricing with sustainable profitability strategies. Furthermore, the data
reveals that the total premiums collected in 2024 amounted to CNY 50,000, although there was a decline from the CNY 16,000
reported in 2023.
Year  UnitsSold
RetentionRate  NewPolicies
PolicyStatistics  TotalRevenue
[HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  90%  14000  [HIDDEN]  55000
The analysis also highlights that the persistency levels have seen a decrease from 2022, where they were recorded at 41.0%,
showcasing the need for insurers to enhance customer engagement initiatives. Additionally, the overall market dynamics
suggest that while profitability remains a priority, maintaining customer trust and satisfaction is equally crucial in navigating the
competitive landscape of life insurance in the Asia-Pacific region.
